summary: ADMIN_EMAIL auto-promote feature VERIFIED end-to-end. All 13 pytest cases passed (100%). Covered all four startup branches (promote, already-admin, user-not-found, empty env), login returning role=admin after promotion, admin endpoint access, full forgot/reset/login recovery pipeline, and all existing regression flows (health, setup/status, CMS reads, inquiries, logout).
passed: Startup branch: promote customer -> admin (demote in DB, restart, log 'Promoted existing user vedabrandssupport@gmail.com to admin via ADMIN_EMAIL env', DB role updated to admin)
Startup branch: already admin (restart with same env, log 'ADMIN_EMAIL user vedabrandssupport@gmail.com already has admin role', no DB write needed)
Startup branch: user not found (ADMIN_EMAIL=nonexistent_user_xyz@example.com, log 'not found in users; will be created when user first registers or uses /admin/setup', backend stays up)
Startup branch: empty ADMIN_EMAIL (no promotion log lines, backend stays up, no error)
POST /api/auth/login with vedabrandssupport@gmail.com / CustomerPass123! -> 200 with role='admin', access_token cookie set
GET /api/auth/me after promoted login -> 200 returns role='admin'
GET /api/admin/overview after promoted login -> 200 with counts dict
Full recovery pipeline: forgot-password -> reset-password (new pw) -> login (new pw) -> role=admin -> /api/admin/overview 200. Original password restored at end of test.
POST /api/inquiries -> 200 ok:true with id (Resend email kicked off in background, ignored by test)
critical_code_review_comments: Implementation is correct and idempotent: re-running startup with the user already as admin only logs and does not write. Email is .strip().lower() so trailing whitespace or case-different ADMIN_EMAIL in env is normalized.
Minor (non-blocking): on_event('startup') is deprecated in newer FastAPI in favor of lifespan; not a bug here.

Minor (non-blocking): the 'will be created when user first registers or uses /admin/setup' log message is slightly misleading because /api/setup/admin is gated by 'no admin exists' — once any admin exists, /setup/admin returns 400. A new user registering via /api/auth/register with email==ADMIN_EMAIL would only be promoted on the NEXT startup. Consider promoting at register/login time as well, or at least clarifying the log message. Not required to ship the prod fix.
test_credentials: vedabrandssupport@gmail.com / CustomerPass123! (role=admin after auto-promote). Stored in /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Used the pre-seeded user vedabrandssupport@gmail.com. One TEST_ inquiry created via /api/inquiries (test_autopromote@example.com); harmless test data. Recovery flow test toggles the password and restores it within the same test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The auto-promote feature is fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_admin_email_promotion.py. TestStartupBranches restarts the backend 4 times and edits /app/backend/.env (always restored). If you re-run this suite, total runtime is ~100s due to those restarts. Final state of the env after the suite: ADMIN_EMAIL=vedabrandssupport@gmail.com (restored). Final state of the DB: user vedabrandssupport@gmail.com has role=admin and password=CustomerPass123! (restored at the end of TestRecoveryFlow). Be aware that ADMIN_EMAIL promotion only runs on startup; runtime registration with the ADMIN_EMAIL email would create a customer until the next restart (see code review comment).
rca_of_the_issue: Original prod bug: vedabrandssupport@gmail.com existed as a customer in prod; forgot-password worked but /admin/login rejected with 'This account is not an admin.' Fix: on FastAPI startup, if ADMIN_EMAIL env var is set, the matching user (if any) is promoted to role='admin' via a single $set on the users collection. Idempotent (no-op when already admin) and crash-safe (logs informatively when user doesn't exist or env is empty). Verified by directly inspecting backend logs and by logging in with the credentials after each restart.
